from manim import *

class ContrastiveAlignment(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create Encoder Blocks
        img_encoder = VGroup(
            RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.1, height=1.5, width=3, color=BLUE),
            Text("Image Encoder", font_size=24)
        ).shift(LEFT * 3.5 + UP * 2.5)

        text_encoder = VGroup(
            RoundedRectangle(corner_radius=0.1, height=1.5, width=3, color=GREEN),
            Text("Text Encoder", font_size=24)
        ).shift(RIGHT * 3.5 + UP * 2.5)

        # 2. Show Inputs
        street_view_img = Square(side_length=1.2, fill_opacity=0.5, color=GRAY).next_to(img_encoder, LEFT, buff=0.5)
        img_placeholder_text = Text("Street\nView", font_size=16).move_to(street_view_img)
        
        france_text = Text("'A Street View photo in France'", font_size=20, color=YELLOW).next_to(text_encoder, RIGHT, buff=0.5)

        self.play(FadeIn(img_encoder, text_encoder, street_view_img, img_placeholder_text, france_text))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Coordinate System (Shared Mathematical Space)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            y_range=[-3, 3, 1],
            x_length=5,
            y_length=5,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).shift(DOWN * 1)
        axes_labels = axes.get_axis_labels(x_label="x", y_label="y")
        space_title = Text("Shared Embedding Space", font_size=24).next_to(axes, UP)

        self.play(Create(axes), Write(axes_labels), Write(space_title))

        # 4. Vectors and Similarity Score
        # ValueTracker for similarity score
        sim_tracker = ValueTracker(0.42)
        
        # Similarity display
        sim_score_label = Text("Cosine Similarity:", font_size=24).to_edge(RIGHT).shift(UP * 0.5)
        sim_score_val = DecimalNumber(sim_tracker.get_value(), num_decimal_places=2, color=YELLOW).next_to(sim_score_label, DOWN)
        sim_score_val.add_updater(lambda d: d.set_value(sim_tracker.get_value()))

        # Define vectors
        # v1: Image Embedding (stays relatively fixed)
        # v2: Text Embedding (will move)
        origin = axes.c2p(0,0)
        img_vec = Arrow(origin, axes.c2p(2, 1, 0), buff=0, color=BLUE)
        img_vec_label = MathTex("z_{img}", color=BLUE, font_size=24).next_to(img_vec.get_end(), UR, buff=0.1)

        text_vec_france = Arrow(origin, axes.c2p(0.5, 2.2, 0), buff=0, color=GREEN)
        text_vec_label = MathTex("z_{text}", color=GREEN, font_size=24).next_to(text_vec_france.get_end(), UP, buff=0.1)

        self.play(GrowArrow(img_vec), Write(img_vec_label))
        self.play(GrowArrow(text_vec_france), Write(text_vec_label))
        self.play(Write(sim_score_label), FadeIn(sim_score_val))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Animate Alignment (Correct Label)
        target_text_vec = Arrow(origin, axes.c2p(1.9, 0.95, 0), buff=0, color=GREEN)
        
        self.play(
            Transform(text_vec_france, target_text_vec),
            text_vec_label.animate.next_to(target_text_vec.get_end(), DR, buff=0.1),
            sim_tracker.animate.set_value(0.98),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(2)

        # 6. Show "Wrong" Label and Pushing Away
        japan_text = Text("'A Street View photo in Japan'", font_size=20, color=RED).move_to(france_text)
        
        self.play(Transform(france_text, japan_text))
        
        # Pushing Japan vector away
        wrong_text_vec = Arrow(origin, axes.c2p(-1.5, 1.8, 0), buff=0, color=RED)
        
        self.play(
            Transform(text_vec_france, wrong_text_vec),
            text_vec_label.animate.next_to(wrong_text_vec.get_end(), UL, buff=0.1),
            text_vec_france.animate.set_color(RED),
            text_vec_label.animate.set_color(RED),
            sim_tracker.animate.set_value(0.15),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(2)

        # 7. Display Loss Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\mathcal{L}_{CLIP} = 0.5 \cdot (\mathcal{L}_{Text} + \mathcal{L}_{Images})",
            font_size=36
        ).to_edge(LEFT).shift(DOWN * 0.5)
        
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(Indicate(formula))
        self.wait(3)

from manim import *

class ConceptAnimation01(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Encoders (Image and Text Blocks)
        img_box = RoundedRectangle(height=1, width=2.2, color=BLUE).shift(UP * 2.5 + LEFT * 3)
        img_text = Text("Image Encoder", font_size=20).move_to(img_box)
        
        txt_box = RoundedRectangle(height=1, width=2.2, color=GREEN).shift(UP * 2.5 + RIGHT * 3)
        txt_text = Text("Text Encoder", font_size=20).move_to(txt_box)
        
        self.add(img_box, img_text, txt_box, txt_text)
        
        # 2. Inputs
        img_in = Square(side_length=0.8, color=WHITE).next_to(img_box, LEFT, buff=0.4)
        img_in_label = Text("Image", font_size=14).move_to(img_in)
        txt_in = Text("'A Street View photo in France'", font_size=16).next_to(txt_box, RIGHT, buff=0.4)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(img_in), FadeIn(img_in_label), Write(txt_in))
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # 3. Shared Embedding Space (Manual Coordinate System)
        # Using simple Arrows instead of Axes to avoid "disallowed" identifiers
        origin_pt = DOWN * 1.5 + LEFT * 1.5
        x_axis = Arrow(origin_pt, origin_pt + RIGHT * 4, buff=0, color=WHITE)
        y_axis = Arrow(origin_pt, origin_pt + UP * 3, buff=0, color=WHITE)
        space_label = Text("Shared Embedding Space", font_size=18).next_to(x_axis, DOWN, buff=0.3)
        
        self.play(Create(x_axis), Create(y_axis), Write(space_label))
        
        # 4. Vectors (Image and Text)
        # Initial separate directions
        vec_img = Arrow(origin_pt, origin_pt + [1, 2, 0], buff=0, color=BLUE)
        vec_txt = Arrow(origin_pt, origin_pt + [2.5, 0.5, 0], buff=0, color=GREEN)
        
        img_label = Text("Image Vec", font_size=14, color=BLUE).next_to(vec_img.get_end(), UP)
        txt_label = Text("France Vec", font_size=14, color=GREEN).next_to(vec_txt.get_end(), RIGHT)
        
        self.play(GrowArrow(vec_img), GrowArrow(vec_txt), Write(img_label), Write(txt_label))
        
        # 5. Similarity Score (Manual updates)
        sim_title = Text("Cosine Similarity: ", font_size=24).to_edge(UP, buff=0.7)
        sim_val = Text("0.35", font_size=24, color=YELLOW).next_to(sim_title, RIGHT)
        self.play(Write(sim_title), Write(sim_val))
        
        # 6. Animate Alignment (Positive Pair)
        target_pt = origin_pt + [2, 2, 0]
        sim_val_aligned = Text("1.00", font_size=24, color=YELLOW).move_to(sim_val)
        
        self.play(
            vec_img.animate.put_start_and_end_on(origin_pt, target_pt),
            vec_txt.animate.put_start_and_end_on(origin_pt, target_pt),
            img_label.animate.move_to(origin_pt + [1.8, 2.3, 0]),
            txt_label.animate.move_to(origin_pt + [2.3, 1.8, 0]),
            ReplacementTransform(sim_val, sim_val_aligned),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)
        
        # 7. Wrong Label (Negative Pair)
        txt_wrong = Text("'A Street View photo in Japan'", font_size=16, color=RED).move_to(txt_in)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(txt_in, txt_wrong))
        
        # Create Japan vector starting near the image
        vec_japan = Arrow(origin_pt, target_pt, buff=0, color=RED)
        japan_label = Text("Japan Vec", font_size=14, color=RED).move_to(txt_label)
        
        self.play(FadeOut(vec_txt), FadeOut(txt_label))
        self.play(FadeIn(vec_japan), Write(japan_label))
        
        # Pushing away (Contrastive Learning)
        away_pt = origin_pt + [3.5, 0.3, 0]
        sim_val_low = Text("0.12", font_size=24, color=YELLOW).move_to(sim_val_aligned)
        
        self.play(
            vec_japan.animate.put_start_and_end_on(origin_pt, away_pt),
            japan_label.animate.next_to(away_pt, RIGHT),
            ReplacementTransform(sim_val_aligned, sim_val_low),
            run_time=2
        )
        self.wait(1)
        
        # 8. Show Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\mathcal{L}_{CLIP} = 0.5 \cdot (\mathcal{L}_{Text} + \mathcal{L}_{Images})",
            color=WHITE
        ).scale(0.8).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.2)
        
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(3)

  "formula": "h(\\mathbf{v};\\mathbf{X})=\\frac{\\exp(\\mathbf{X}\\mathbf{v})}{\\sum_{j=1}^{N}\\exp(\\mathbf{X}_{j}^{T}\\mathbf{v})}",

}

from manim import *

class ZeroShotClassification(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Formula setup
        formula = MathTex(
            r"h(\mathbf{v};\mathbf{X})=\frac{\exp(\mathbf{X}\mathbf{v})}{\sum_{j=1}^{N}\exp(\mathbf{X}_{j}^{T}\mathbf{v})}",
            font_size=36
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)

        # 2. Image Vector v
        v_rect = Rectangle(height=3.5, width=0.7, color=BLUE).set_fill(BLUE, opacity=0.3)
        v_label = MathTex(r"\mathbf{v}").move_to(v_rect.get_center())
        v_desc = Text("Image Representation", font_size=18).next_to(v_rect, DOWN)
        v_group = VGroup(v_rect, v_label, v_desc).shift(LEFT * 5)

        # 3. Matrix X (Text Embeddings)
        countries = ["France", "Japan", "USA", "Brazil", "Canada", "Germany", "Others..."]
        x_rows = VGroup(*[
            VGroup(
                Rectangle(height=0.4, width=3.2, stroke_width=1).set_fill(GREY_D, opacity=0.2),
                Text(country, font_size=18)
            )
            for country in countries
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.1)
        
        x_label = MathTex(r"\mathbf{X}").next_to(x_rows, UP, buff=0.3)
        x_desc = Text("Text Embeddings Matrix", font_size=18).next_to(x_rows, DOWN)
        x_group = VGroup(x_rows, x_label, x_desc).shift(LEFT * 1)

        # 4. Manual Bar Chart Setup (to avoid BarChart component issues)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 7, 1],
            y_range=[0, 1, 0.5],
            x_length=4.5,
            y_length=3,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False, "font_size": 20}
        ).shift(RIGHT * 4 + DOWN * 0.5)
        
        chart_title = Text("Softmax Probabilities", font_size=20).next_to(axes, UP)
        
        # Create bars as individual rectangles
        initial_vals = [0.1, 0.08, 0.12, 0.05, 0.1, 0.05, 0.05]
        bars = VGroup()
        for i, val in enumerate(initial_vals):
            bar = Rectangle(
                width=0.4, 
                height=val * axes.y_axis.get_length(),
                fill_opacity=0.8,
                color=YELLOW
            ).move_to(axes.c2p(i + 0.5, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN)
            bars.add(bar)

        # 5. Animation Sequence
        
        # Introduce v and X
        self.play(FadeIn(v_group))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(FadeIn(x_group))
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Show formula
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # Pulsing dot product highlight moving through matrix X
        highlight = Rectangle(
            height=0.45, width=3.4, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=4
        ).move_to(x_rows[0].get_center())

        self.play(Create(highlight))
        for i in range(1, 4):
            self.play(highlight.animate.move_to(x_rows[i].get_center()), run_time=0.3)
        self.play(FadeOut(highlight))

        # Transition to Calculation
        dot_product_label = MathTex(r"\mathbf{X} \cdot \mathbf{v}", color=YELLOW).next_to(x_group, RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(dot_product_label))
        self.play(dot_product_label.animate.move_to(formula.get_center()))
        self.play(FadeOut(dot_product_label))

        # Show Bar Chart axes
        self.play(Create(axes), Write(chart_title))
        self.play(Create(bars))
        self.wait(1)

        # Softmax update: France (index 0) shoots up, others drop
        target_vals = [0.9, 0.02, 0.01, 0.01, 0.03, 0.01, 0.02]
        
        bar_animations = []
        for i, target_val in enumerate(target_vals):
            target_height = target_val * axes.y_axis.get_length()
            # Animate the height and keep the bottom edge at the x-axis
            bar_anim = bars[i].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(target_height, about_edge=DOWN)
            bar_animations.append(bar_anim)
            if i == 0:
                bar_animations.append(bars[i].animate.set_color(GREEN))

        self.play(*bar_animations, run_time=2, rate_func=smooth)
        
        # Final prediction focus
        self.play(Indicate(bars[0], color=GREEN))
        prediction = Text("Prediction: France", color=GREEN, font_size=24).next_to(axes, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(prediction))
        
        self.wait(3)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ZeroShotClassificationConcept(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Image Vector v
        v_label = MathTex(r"\mathbf{v}", color=GREEN).scale(1.2)
        v_rect = Rectangle(height=3, width=0.6, color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.3)
        v_group = VGroup(v_rect, v_label).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1.5)
        v_name = Text("Image Embedding", font_size=24).next_to(v_group, DOWN)

        # 2. Matrix X
        matrix_label = MathTex(r"\mathbf{X}", color=BLUE).scale(1.2)
        x_rect = Rectangle(height=4, width=3, color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.1)
        
        # Rows in Matrix X (Country names)
        countries = ["France", "Japan", "USA", "Brazil", "Canada", "India"]
        country_labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(name, font_size=20) for name in countries
        ]).arrange(DOWN, buff=0.35).move_to(x_rect.get_center())
        
        x_group = VGroup(x_rect, country_labels, matrix_label.next_to(x_rect, UP))
        x_group.to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1.5)
        x_name = Text("Country Text Embeddings", font_size=24).next_to(x_rect, DOWN)

        # 3. Introduction
        title = Text("Zero-Shot Classification", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(title))
        self.play(Create(v_group), Write(v_name))
        self.play(Create(x_group), Write(x_name))
        self.wait()

        # 4. Dot Product Operation (Pulsing Highlight)
        highlight_row = Rectangle(
            height=0.5, width=3.2, color=YELLOW, stroke_width=4
        ).move_to(country_labels[0].get_center())
        
        dot_expr = MathTex(r"\text{score}_i = \mathbf{X}_i \cdot \mathbf{v}").to_edge(UP, buff=1.2)

        self.play(FadeIn(highlight_row), Write(dot_expr))
        
        for i in range(len(countries)):
            target_pos = country_labels[i].get_center()
            self.play(highlight_row.animate.move_to(target_pos), run_time=0.2)
            self.play(highlight_row.animate.set_stroke(width=8), run_time=0.1)
            self.play(highlight_row.animate.set_stroke(width=4), run_time=0.1)

        self.play(FadeOut(highlight_row))

        # 5. Softmax Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"h(\mathbf{v};\mathbf{X}) = \frac{\exp(\mathbf{X}\mathbf{v})}{\sum_{j=1}^{N}\exp(\mathbf{X}_{j}^{T}\mathbf{v})}"
        ).scale(0.9).to_edge(UP, buff=1.0)
        
        self.play(Transform(dot_expr, formula))
        self.wait()

        # 6. Manual Bar Chart using Axes (More compatible than BarChart)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 6, 1],
            y_range=[0, 1, 0.5],
            x_length=7,
            y_length=3,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False}
        ).shift(DOWN * 0.5)
        
        labels = VGroup(*[
            Text(countries[i][:3], font_size=18).next_to(axes.c2p(i + 0.5, 0), DOWN)
            for i in range(len(countries))
        ])

        # Initial flat bars
        bars = VGroup(*[
            Rectangle(
                width=0.6, 
                height=0.1, 
                fill_color=BLUE, 
                fill_opacity=0.7, 
                stroke_width=1
            ).move_to(axes.c2p(i + 0.5, 0), aligned_edge=DOWN)
            for i in range(len(countries))
        ])

        self.play(
            FadeOut(v_group, v_name, x_group, x_name),
            Create(axes),
            Create(labels),
            Create(bars)
        )

        # Update bars to show France (index 0) shooting up
        new_heights = [2.5, 0.1, 0.05, 0.15, 0.1, 0.1] # Scaled to y_length
        
        animations = []
        for i in range(len(bars)):
            animations.append(
                bars[i].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(new_heights[i], about_edge=DOWN)
            )
            if i == 0:
                animations[-1] = bars[i].animate.stretch_to_fit_height(new_heights[i], about_edge=DOWN).set_color(YELLOW)

        self.play(*animations, run_time=2, rate_func=smooth)
        
        # Highlight winner
        winner_label = Text("Prediction: France", color=YELLOW, font_size=32).next_to(axes, UP, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(winner_label))
        self.play(Indicate(bars[0], color=YELLOW))
        
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class ZeroShotClassification(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Image and Visual Vector v
        # Using a square as a placeholder if the image is missing
        try:
            image = ImageMobject("img-8.jpeg").scale(1.2).to_corner(UL, buff=0.5)
        except:
            image = Rectangle(height=2, width=3, color=BLUE).to_corner(UL, buff=0.5)
            image.add(Text("Image", font_size=20).move_to(image))

        # Vector v representation
        v_vector = MathTex(
            r"\mathbf{v} = \begin{bmatrix} 0.1 \\ 0.5 \\ \vdots \\ -0.2 \end{bmatrix}",
            color=YELLOW, font_size=32
        ).next_to(image, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        v_desc = Text("Image Representation", font_size=20).next_to(v_vector, DOWN, buff=0.2)

        # 2. Matrix X representation
        x_matrix = MathTex(
            r"\mathbf{X} = \begin{bmatrix} \text{France} \\ \text{Germany} \\ \vdots \\ \text{Japan} \end{bmatrix}",
            color=BLUE, font_size=32
        ).next_to(v_vector, RIGHT, buff=1.5)
        x_desc = Text("Country Embeddings", font_size=20).next_to(x_matrix, DOWN, buff=0.2)

        # 3. Probability Visualization (Manual Bar Chart for better compatibility)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 4, 1],
            y_range=[0, 1, 0.5],
            x_length=4,
            y_length=2,
            axis_config={"include_tip": False},
            tips=False
        ).to_corner(UR, buff=1.0)
        
        labels = VGroup(
            Text("DE", font_size=16).next_to(axes.c2p(0.5, 0), DOWN),
            Text("UK", font_size=16).next_to(axes.c2p(1.5, 0), DOWN),
            Text("FR", font_size=16).next_to(axes.c2p(2.5, 0), DOWN),
            Text("JP", font_size=16).next_to(axes.c2p(3.5, 0), DOWN)
        )

        def get_bars(values):
            bars = VGroup()
            for i, val in enumerate(values):
                bar = Rectangle(
                    width=0.6, 
                    height=val * 2, 
                    fill_opacity=0.8, 
                    fill_color=GREEN if i == 2 else BLUE,
                    stroke_width=1
                )
                bar.move_to(axes.c2p(i + 0.5, val / 2))
                bars.add(bar)
            return bars

        initial_bars = get_bars([0.1, 0.1, 0.1, 0.1])
        final_bars = get_bars([0.05, 0.1, 0.8, 0.05])

        # 4. Mathematical Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"h(\mathbf{v};\mathbf{X}) = \frac{\exp(\mathbf{X}\mathbf{v})}{\sum_{j=1}^{N}\exp(\mathbf{X}_{j}^{T}\mathbf{v})}",
            font_size=36
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.8)

        # --- ANIMATION ---
        
        # Introduction
        intro_text = Text("Zero-Shot Classifier", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.play(FadeIn(image), Write(intro_text))
        self.wait(1)

        # Show Vector v and Matrix X
        self.play(Write(v_vector), Write(v_desc))
        self.play(Write(x_matrix), Write(x_desc))
        self.wait(1)

        # Pulse operation logic
        highlight_rect = SurroundingRectangle(x_matrix, color=YELLOW, buff=0.1)
        self.play(Create(highlight_rect))
        self.play(FadeOut(highlight_rect))

        # Show Equation
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # Probabilities Updating
        self.play(Create(axes), Create(labels))
        self.play(Create(initial_bars))
        self.wait(0.5)
        self.play(Transform(initial_bars, final_bars), run_time=2)
        
        # Result Highlight
        prediction = Text("Prediction: France", font_size=28, color=GREEN).next_to(axes, DOWN, buff=0.8)
        self.play(Write(prediction))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np
import random

class ConceptAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Manual Coordinate System (Replacing Axes/NumberPlane)
        x_axis = Line(LEFT * 5, RIGHT * 5, color=WHITE, stroke_width=1)
        y_axis = Line(DOWN * 3, UP * 3, color=WHITE, stroke_width=1)
        coords = VGroup(x_axis, y_axis)

        # 2. Country Outline (Simplified Australia shape)
        country_points = [
            [-3, 0.5, 0], [-2, 1.5, 0], [1, 1.2, 0], [3, 0.5, 0], 
            [3.5, -1.5, 0], [1, -2, 0], [-1.5, -2, 0], [-3, -1, 0]
        ]
        country = Polygon(*country_points, color=YELLOW, fill_opacity=0.1)

        # 3. Data points (gray dots)
        num_points = 60
        dots = VGroup()
        for _ in range(num_points):
            px = random.uniform(-2.5, 2.5)
            py = random.uniform(-1.5, 1.0)
            dots.add(Dot(point=[px, py, 0], color=GRAY, radius=0.07))

        # 4. Custom Cross Function (Replacing Cross)
        def get_cross(pos, color):
            size = 0.2
            l1 = Line(pos + UL * size, pos + DR * size, color=color, stroke_width=6)
            l2 = Line(pos + UR * size, pos + DL * size, color=color, stroke_width=6)
            return VGroup(l1, l2)

        # Centroids setup
        centroid_colors = [RED, GREEN, BLUE]
        centroid_positions = [
            [1.5, 1.5, 0],
            [-2.0, -1.0, 0],
            [0.5, -2.0, 0]
        ]
        centroids = VGroup(*[
            get_cross(pos, centroid_colors[i]) 
            for i, pos in enumerate(centroid_positions)
        ])

        # Formula and Labels
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\mu_{j}:=\frac{\sum_{i=1}^{n}\mathbf{1}\{c^{(i)}=j\}x^{(i)}}{\sum_{i=1}^{n}\mathbf{1}\{c^{(i)}=j\}}",
            font_size=36
        ).to_edge(UP)
        
        label_data = Text("Continuous Coordinates", font_size=24).to_edge(LEFT, buff=0.5).shift(UP*2.5)
        label_target = Text("Discrete Regions", font_size=24, color=YELLOW).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=0.5).shift(UP*2.5)

        # --- ANIMATION ---

        # Show Initial Space
        self.play(Create(coords), Create(country))
        self.play(FadeIn(dots, lag_ratio=0.05), Write(label_data))
        self.wait(1)

        # Introduce Centroids and Formula
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(AnimationGroup(*[Create(c) for c in centroids], lag_ratio=0.2))
        self.wait(1)

        # Assignment Step
        coloring_anims = []
        clusters = [[] for _ in range(len(centroids))]
        
        for dot in dots:
            dot_pos = dot.get_center()
            distances = [np.linalg.norm(dot_pos - c.get_center()) for c in centroids]
            nearest_idx = np.argmin(distances)
            clusters[nearest_idx].append(dot)
            coloring_anims.append(dot.animate.set_color(centroid_colors[nearest_idx]))
        
        self.play(*coloring_anims, run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

        # Update Step (Move centroids to mean of their clusters)
        move_anims = []
        for i, cluster_dots in enumerate(clusters):
            if cluster_dots:
                positions = [d.get_center() for d in cluster_dots]
                mean_pos = np.mean(positions, axis=0)
                move_anims.append(centroids[i].animate.move_to(mean_pos))

        self.play(FadeOut(label_data), Write(label_target))
        self.play(*move_anims, run_time=2)
        
        # Highlight final targets
        self.play(
            *[c.animate.scale(1.3) for c in centroids]
        )
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ConceptAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Image Input
        # Use a placeholder since the image file may not exist
        image_placeholder = Rectangle(height=2, width=3, color=WHITE).set_fill(GREY, opacity=0.5)
        image_text = Text("Image Input", font_size=24).move_to(image_placeholder.get_center())
        image_group = VGroup(image_placeholder, image_text).to_edge(LEFT, buff=1)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(image_group))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 2. CLIP Block and Country MLP Identification
        clip_block = VGroup(
            Rectangle(width=2, height=1, color=BLUE),
            Text("CLIP", font_size=30)
        ).next_to(image_group, RIGHT, buff=1)
        
        country_text = Text("Country MLP", color=BLUE, font_size=24).next_to(clip_block, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        arrow_to_clip = Arrow(image_group.get_right(), clip_block.get_left(), buff=0.1)
        
        self.play(Create(arrow_to_clip), FadeIn(clip_block))
        self.play(Write(country_text))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. 3-Layer Neural Network (MLP)
        self.play(FadeOut(image_group, clip_block, arrow_to_clip, country_text))
        
        layer_sizes = [4, 6, 4]
        layers = VGroup()
        for size in layer_sizes:
            layer = VGroup(*[Dot(radius=0.1, color=YELLOW) for _ in range(size)])
            layer.arrange(DOWN, buff=0.3)
            layers.add(layer)
        layers.arrange(RIGHT, buff=1.5)
        layers.move_to(ORIGIN)

        connections = VGroup()
        for i in range(len(layers) - 1):
            for dot1 in layers[i]:
                for dot2 in layers[i+1]:
                    line = Line(dot1.get_center(), dot2.get_center(), stroke_width=1, stroke_opacity=0.3, color=WHITE)
                    connections.add(line)

        mlp_label = Text("Local Region MLP (ReLU)", font_size=30).to_edge(UP)
        
        self.play(Create(layers), Create(connections), Write(mlp_label))

        # 4. Animate signals flowing through the MLP
        for i in range(len(layers) - 1):
            signals = VGroup(*[
                Dot(point=dot.get_center(), radius=0.05, color=GOLD)
                for dot in layers[i]
            ])
            self.play(
                signals.animate.move_to(layers[i+1].get_center()),
                run_time=0.8,
                rate_func=linear
            )
            self.remove(signals)

        # 5. Formula transition
        formula = MathTex(r"c^{(i)}:=\arg\min_{j}\|x^{(i)}-\mu_{j}\|^{2}").to_edge(DOWN, buff=1)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Map and GPS Pin
        self.play(FadeOut(layers, connections, mlp_label, formula))
        
        country_map = Circle(radius=2, color=GREEN).set_fill(GREEN, opacity=0.2)
        map_label = Text("Specific Country Region", font_size=24).next_to(country_map, UP)
        
        centroids = VGroup(*[
            Dot(color=RED).move_to(
                country_map.get_center() + np.array([np.cos(a), np.sin(a), 0]) * 1.2
            ) for a in [0, 1.2, 2.5, 4, 5.5]
        ])
        target_centroid = centroids[2]
        
        self.play(Create(country_map), Write(map_label), Create(centroids))
        
        # Create a GPS Pin from scratch
        pin_head = Circle(radius=0.15, color=RED).set_fill(RED, opacity=1)
        pin_tip = Triangle(color=RED).scale(0.15).rotate(180*DEGREES).set_fill(RED, opacity=1)
        pin_tip.next_to(pin_head, DOWN, buff=-0.05)
        pin = VGroup(pin_head, pin_tip)
        pin.next_to(target_centroid, UP, buff=0)
        
        self.play(target_centroid.animate.scale(1.5).set_color(YELLOW))
        self.play(FadeIn(pin, shift=DOWN))
        self.wait(1)

        # 7. Transition Image Vector to Coordinates
        # Use ReplacementTransform since TransformMatchingTex failed
        vec_start = MathTex(r"\vec{x}_{img} = [v_1, v_2, \dots, v_n]").to_edge(UP)
        vec_end = MathTex(r"(\text{lat}, \text{lon}) = (48.85, 2.35)").to_edge(UP)
        
        self.play(Write(vec_start))
        self.wait(1)
        self.play(ReplacementTransform(vec_start, vec_end))
        self.wait(1)

        # 8. Narration final text
        narration_text = Text(
            "Hierarchical approach: from global country to local region refinement.",
            font_size=20
        ).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        
        self.play(Write(narration_text))
        self.wait(3)

from manim import *

class ConceptScene02OpponentAbstraction(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Create the 2-player game board
        n = 3  # For 2 players
        opponent1 = Circle(color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.5)
        opponent2 = Circle(color=RED, fill_opacity=0.5)
        board_2player = VGroup(opponent1, opponent2).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Create(board_2player))
        
        # Narration
        text_2player = Text("A 2-player board with two distinct colors:")
        self.play(Write(text_2player), run_time=1)
        
        # Create the 6-player game board
        n = 3  # For 6 players (5 opponents + "Red" layer)
        opponent1 = Circle(color=BLUE, fill_opacity=0.5)
        opponent2 = Circle(color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.5)
        opponent3 = Circle(color=YELLOW, fill_opacity=0.5)
        opponent4 = Circle(color=RED, fill_opacity=0.5)
        opponent5 = Circle(color=BLACK, fill_opacity=0.5)
        # "Red" layer
        opponent6 = Circle(color=RED, fill_opacity=0.5)
        
        board_6player = VGroup(opponent1, opponent2, opponent3, opponent4, opponent5, opponent6).arrange(RIGHT, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Create(board_6player), run_time=1)
        
        # Narration
        text_6player = Text("A 6-player board with 5 different opponent colors:")
        self.play(Write(text_6player), run_time=1)
        
        # Merge the 5 opponent colors into a single 'Red' intensity
        merge_animation = ReplacementTransform(VGroup(opponent1, opponent2, opponent3, opponent4, opponent5), opponent6)
        self.play(merge_animation, run_time=1)
        
        # Cross-out the individual identities of the opponents
        for opponent in VGroup(opponent1, opponent2, opponent3, opponent4, opponent5):
            self.play(Indicate(opponent), run_time=0.5)
        # Check mark over the unified 'Opponent Layer'
        check_mark = Text("Check")
        check_mark.scale(1.5)
        check_mark.set_color(WHITE)
        check_mark.to_edge(DOWN, buff=1.5)
        
        self.play(Write(check_mark), run_time=1)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class DualRewardShaping(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create the game board and target zone
        board = Rectangle(width=10, height=6, color=WHITE)
        target_zone = Rectangle(width=2, height=4, color=YELLOW, fill_opacity=0.2).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1)
        target_label = Text("Target Zone", color=YELLOW, font_size=24).next_to(target_zone, UP)
        self.add(board, target_zone, target_label)

        # 2. Add pegs to the board in a cluster
        pegs = VGroup(*[
            Dot(color=BLUE, radius=0.15)
            for _ in range(6)
        ])
        for i, peg in enumerate(pegs):
            peg.move_to([-4 + (i % 2) * 0.5, -1 + (i // 2) * 0.8, 0])
        self.add(pegs)

        # 3. Setup Centroid and Target Marker
        def get_centroid_pos():
            return np.mean([p.get_center() for p in pegs], axis=0)

        centroid = Dot(get_centroid_pos(), color=RED)
        centroid_text = Text("Centroid", color=RED, font_size=20)
        
        # Line from Centroid to Target Center
        target_center_point = target_zone.get_center()
        dist_line = Line(centroid.get_center(), target_center_point, color=WHITE)
        
        self.add(centroid, centroid_text, dist_line)

        # Updaters for movement
        centroid.add_updater(lambda m: m.move_to(get_centroid_pos()))
        centroid_text.add_updater(lambda m: m.next_to(centroid, UP, buff=0.1))
        dist_line.add_updater(lambda m: m.put_start_and_end_on(centroid.get_center(), target_center_point))

        # 4. Reward Display and Formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"R = w_1(p_{in})^2 + w_2(dist)^{-1}",
            font_size=30
        ).to_edge(UP)
        
        reward_tex = MathTex("Reward: 0.00", color=GREEN).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        self.add(formula, reward_tex)

        # 5. Animation: Pegs moving forward
        pegs_in_count = 0
        for i in range(len(pegs)):
            # Move peg into target zone
            target_pos = target_zone.get_center() + [
                np.random.uniform(-0.4, 0.4),
                np.random.uniform(-1.2, 1.2),
                0
            ]
            
            self.play(pegs[i].animate.move_to(target_pos), run_time=0.8)
            
            # Check if peg is in target (simple x-coordinate check)
            if pegs[i].get_center()[0] > target_zone.get_left()[0]:
                pegs_in_count += 1
                
                # Show squared bonus popup
                bonus_popup = MathTex(f"{pegs_in_count}^2", color=GREEN, font_size=36)
                bonus_popup.next_to(pegs[i], UP, buff=0.2)
                
                # Update Reward Text manually
                dist = np.linalg.norm(get_centroid_pos() - target_center_point)
                current_reward = (pegs_in_count**2) + (5.0 / (dist + 0.5))
                new_reward_tex = MathTex(f"Reward: {current_reward:.2f}", color=GREEN).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
                
                self.play(
                    FadeIn(bonus_popup, shift=UP*0.3),
                    reward_tex.become(new_reward_tex),
                    run_time=0.4
                )
                self.play(FadeOut(bonus_popup), run_time=0.2)

        # Finish
        self.wait(2)
        centroid.clear_updaters()
        centroid_text.clear_updaters()
        dist_line.clear_updaters()

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class DualRewardShaping(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup board and target zone
        board = Rectangle(width=10, height=6, color=WHITE)
        target_zone = Rectangle(width=3, height=6, color=GREEN, fill_opacity=0.2)
        target_zone.align_to(board, RIGHT)
        target_label = Text("Target Zone", font_size=20).next_to(target_zone, UP, buff=0.2)
        self.add(board, target_zone, target_label)

        # 2. Setup pegs (Team of pieces)
        pegs = VGroup()
        for _ in range(4):
            # Using random positions on the left side of the board
            x_pos = -4.5 + np.random.rand() * 2.0
            y_pos = -2.0 + np.random.rand() * 4.0
            p = Dot(point=[x_pos, y_pos, 0], color=BLUE)
            pegs.add(p)
        self.add(pegs)

        # 3. Centroid indicator with updaters
        centroid_dot = Dot(color=RED).scale(1.3)
        centroid_label = Text("Centroid", font_size=18, color=RED)
        
        def update_centroid_dot(mob):
            pos_list = []
            for p in pegs:
                pos_list.append(p.get_center())
            avg_pos = np.mean(pos_list, axis=0)
            mob.move_to(avg_pos)

        def update_centroid_label(mob):
            mob.next_to(centroid_dot, UP, buff=0.1)

        centroid_dot.add_updater(update_centroid_dot)
        centroid_label.add_updater(update_centroid_label)
        self.add(centroid_dot, centroid_label)

        # 4. Connection Line to Target (Standard Line used to ensure compatibility)
        target_line = Line(color=YELLOW)
        def update_target_line(mob):
            c_p = centroid_dot.get_center()
            t_x = target_zone.get_left()[0]
            # Line from centroid horizontal to target zone boundary
            mob.put_start_and_end_on(c_p, [t_x, c_p[1], 0])

        target_line.add_updater(update_target_line)
        self.add(target_line)

        # 5. Reward Counter display using Text updates
        reward_text = Text("Reward: 0.00", font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
        self.add(reward_text)

        def update_reward_text(mob):
            c_p = centroid_dot.get_center()
            t_p = target_zone.get_center()
            # Distance between centroid and target center
            dist = np.linalg.norm(c_p - t_p)
            
            # Count pegs that have crossed into the target zone
            count = 0
            for p in pegs:
                if p.get_center()[0] > target_zone.get_left()[0]:
                    count += 1
            
            # Reward Formula: R = w1 * (pegs_in)^2 + w2 * (dist)^-1
            w1 = 2.0
            w2 = 10.0
            # Small constant 0.1 added to distance to avoid division by zero
            val = w1 * (count ** 2) + w2 * (1.0 / (dist + 0.1))
            
            # Create new text for content change
            new_mob = Text("Reward: " + str(round(val, 2)), font_size=32).to_edge(UP)
            mob.become(new_mob)

        reward_text.add_updater(update_reward_text)

        # 6. Movement Animation
        for i in range(len(pegs)):
            peg = pegs[i]
            # Move individual pegs one by one into the target zone
            target_x = target_zone.get_left()[0] + 0.5 + i * 0.3
            target_y = -2.0 + np.random.rand() * 4.0
            
            self.play(
                peg.animate.move_to([target_x, target_y, 0]),
                run_time=1.5
            )
            
            # Squared bonus pop-up animation
            bonus_str = str(i + 1) + "^2"
            bonus_txt = Text(bonus_str, font_size=24, color=YELLOW).next_to(peg, UP)
            self.play(FadeIn(bonus_txt), run_time=0.4)
            self.play(FadeOut(bonus_txt), run_time=0.4)

        # 7. Formula display at the end
        formula = Text("R = w1(pegs_in)^2 + w2(dist)^-1", font_size=24).to_edge(DOWN, buff=0.5)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(2)

{
  "id": "concept-01-gravnet-message-passing",
  "voice": "The LDMX calorimeter has an irregular geometry, so we treat every hit as a node in a graph. GravNet works by creating a local neighborhood around each hit. The key is that the strength of the connection between hits isn't fixed; it's weighted by a potential that depends on their distance in a learned feature space. This allows the network to dynamically decide which energy deposits belong to the same particle shower, effectively 'feeling' the shape of the energy deposit as it develops through the detector.",
  "goal": "Explain how GravNet uses distance-weighted graph connections to process irregular calorimeter data.",
  "remarks": "Create a 3D scatter plot of dots representing ECal cells in an irregular hexagonal-like grid. Highlight one central dot. Draw arrows from the central dot to its neighbors. Animate the arrows' opacity and thickness scaling with the function exp(-d^2), where d is the distance. As the 'learned features' update, show the dots moving slightly and the connections shifting dynamically to emphasize that the graph structure is not fixed by physical geometry but by learned relationships.",
  "formula": "V_{jk} = \\exp(-d_{jk}^2)",
  "image_ref": "img-0.jpeg"
}
```

Use the 'voice' for narration, 'goal' for the objective, and 'remarks' for the detailed animation blueprint.

Also, use this formula as a reference:
V_{jk} = \exp(-d_{jk}^2)
generated_code:
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class GravNetMessagePassing(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Create an irregular grid of dots
        # We simulate a 3D-like perspective by projecting 3D coords to 2D
        dots = VGroup()
        points = []
        for row in range(-2, 3):
            for col in range(-2, 3):
                # Hexagonal grid base
                x = col * 1.2 + (0.6 if row % 2 else 0)
                y = row * 1.0
                # Add some irregularity
                pos = np.array([
                    x + np.random.uniform(-0.3, 0.3),
                    y + np.random.uniform(-0.3, 0.3),
                    0
                ])
                points.append(pos)
                dots.add(Dot(point=pos, color=RED, radius=0.08))
        
        # Highlight a central dot
        central_index = 12
        central_dot = dots[central_index]
        central_dot.set_color(GREEN)

        # 2. Formula and Text
        formula = MathTex(r"V_{jk} = \exp(-d_{jk}^2)")
        formula.to_corner(UL).scale(1.2)
        
        voiceover_text = Text(
            "GravNet: Distance-weighted graph connections", 
            font_size=24
        ).to_edge(DOWN)

        # 3. Create connections that update dynamically
        connections = VGroup()

        def get_weighted_connections(vg):
            vg.submobjects = []
            center_pos = central_dot.get_center()
            for dot in dots:
                if dot == central_dot:
                    continue
                
                target_pos = dot.get_center()
                dist = np.linalg.norm(target_pos - center_pos)
                
                # Use a threshold for neighborhood
                if dist < 3.5:
                    # Connection strength based on distance formula
                    weight = np.exp(-(dist**2) / 2.0)
                    
                    line = Line(
                        center_pos, target_pos,
                        stroke_width=weight * 8,
                        stroke_opacity=weight,
                        color=WHITE,
                        buff=0.1
                    )
                    # Add tip to make it an arrow
                    line.add_tip(tip_length=0.15 * weight, tip_width=0.15 * weight)
                    vg.add(line)

        connections.add_updater(get_weighted_connections)

        # 4. Animation Sequence
        self.add(dots, formula, connections, voiceover_text)
        self.play(Create(dots), Write(formula), FadeIn(voiceover_text))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Move dots to simulate "learned features" updating
        # This causes the connections to shift dynamically via the updater
        move_anims = []
        for dot in dots:
            # Shift dots randomly to represent the feature space evolution
            shift_vector = np.array([
                np.random.uniform(-1.0, 1.0),
                np.random.uniform(-1.0, 1.0),
                0
            ])
            move_anims.append(dot.animate.shift(shift_vector))

        update_desc = Text(
            "Learned features shift the graph structure...", 
            font_size=24
        ).to_edge(DOWN)

        self.play(
            Transform(voiceover_text, update_desc),
            *move_anims,
            run_time=4,
            rate_func=smooth
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Final explanation text
        conclusion = Text(
            "Connection strength is dynamic, not fixed by geometry.", 
            font_size=24
        ).to_edge(DOWN)
        self.play(Transform(voiceover_text, conclusion))
        self.wait(2)

        # 6. Optional Image handling
        try:
            image = ImageMobject("img-0.jpeg")
            image.scale(0.5).to_corner(DR)
            self.play(FadeIn(image))
            self.wait(2)
        except:
            # Skip if file not found
            pass

    def get_image_ref(self):
        return "img-0.jpeg"

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class GravNetMessagePassing(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Formula Display
        formula = MathTex(r"V_{jk} = \exp(-d_{jk}^2)").to_corner(UL)
        self.add(formula)

        # 2. Create an irregular grid of dots
        # Using a 2D approach since 3D features were restricted
        dots = VGroup()
        rows, cols = 5, 5
        points = []
        for r in range(rows):
            for c in range(cols):
                # Hexagonal-like grid spacing
                x = (c + 0.5 * (r % 2)) * 1.4 - 3.0
                y = r * 1.0 - 2.0
                # Adding some jitter for "irregularity"
                x += np.random.uniform(-0.15, 0.15)
                y += np.random.uniform(-0.15, 0.15)
                points.append([x, y, 0])
        
        for p in points:
            dots.add(Dot(point=p, radius=0.08, color=GRAY_B))

        # Identify central dot
        central_idx = len(dots) // 2
        central_dot = dots[central_idx]
        
        self.play(FadeIn(dots))
        self.play(central_dot.animate.set_color(RED).scale(1.4))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Helper for distance-weighted styling
        def get_line_style(p1, p2):
            dist = np.linalg.norm(p1 - p2)
            # The formula exp(-d^2). We scale d slightly for better visuals in screen space.
            weight = np.exp(-(dist**2) / 2.0)
            return {
                "stroke_width": weight * 12,
                "stroke_opacity": weight
            }

        # 4. Create connections (lines) with updaters
        connections = VGroup()
        for i, dot in enumerate(dots):
            if i == central_idx:
                continue
            
            # Initial style
            style = get_line_style(central_dot.get_center(), dot.get_center())
            line = Line(
                central_dot.get_center(), 
                dot.get_center(), 
                color=YELLOW,
                buff=0.1
            ).set_stroke(width=style["stroke_width"], opacity=style["stroke_opacity"])
            
            # Dynamic updater to respond to moving dots
            def line_updater(obj, d_node=dot):
                p1 = central_dot.get_center()
                p2 = d_node.get_center()
                s = get_line_style(p1, p2)
                obj.put_start_and_end_on(p1, p2)
                obj.set_stroke(width=s["stroke_width"], opacity=s["stroke_opacity"])
            
            line.add_updater(line_updater)
            connections.add(line)

        self.play(Create(connections), run_time=2)
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Animate "learned features" updating by moving dots
        # This will trigger the updaters on the connections
        move_animations = []
        for dot in dots:
            # Move each dot to a new nearby random position
            random_offset = np.array([
                np.random.uniform(-1.0, 1.0),
                np.random.uniform(-1.0, 1.0),
                0
            ])
            move_animations.append(dot.animate.shift(random_offset))

        self.play(
            *move_animations,
            run_time=4,
            rate_func=smooth
        )
        
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Highlight the final dynamic state
        self.play(Indicate(central_dot, color=RED))
        self.wait(2)

As the 'learned features' update, show the dots moving slightly and the connections shifting dynamically to emphasize that the graph structure is not fixed by physical geometry but by learned relationships.",

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class GravNetMessagePassing(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Configuration
        n_dots = 40
        
        # Create a set of dots in an irregular/jittered layout
        dots = VGroup()
        for i in range(n_dots):
            # Start in a randomish circular area to represent physical space
            angle = np.random.uniform(0, 2 * PI)
            radius = np.random.uniform(0, 3.5)
            pos = np.array([radius * np.cos(angle), radius * np.sin(angle), 0])
            dot = Dot(point=pos, color=BLUE, radius=0.1)
            dots.add(dot)

        # Select a central dot to focus on
        center_idx = 0
        center_dot = dots[center_idx]
        center_dot.set_color(RED)
        center_dot.set_z_index(10)

        # Formula and Title
        formula = MathTex(r"V_{jk} = \exp(-d_{jk}^2)").to_corner(UL)
        self.add(formula)

        # Function to update line properties based on distance
        def update_connection(line, target_dot):
            p1 = center_dot.get_center()
            p2 = target_dot.get_center()
            dist = np.linalg.norm(p1 - p2)
            # Distance scaling for visualization: 
            # d=0 -> weight=1, d=3 -> weight ~0.1
            weight = np.exp(-(dist / 2.0)**2)
            
            line.put_start_and_end_on(p1, p2)
            line.set_stroke(width=weight * 12, opacity=weight)

        # Create connections (Lines)
        connections = VGroup()
        for i, dot in enumerate(dots):
            if i == center_idx:
                continue
            
            line = Line(center_dot.get_center(), dot.get_center(), color=YELLOW)
            # Use a closure to capture the specific dot for the updater
            line.add_updater(lambda l, d=dot: update_connection(l, d))
            connections.add(line)

        # Animation Sequence
        self.play(FadeIn(dots))
        self.wait(0.5)
        
        # Highlight the central dot and draw connections
        self.play(
            center_dot.animate.scale(1.5),
            Create(connections),
            run_time=1.5
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # Animation: Transition to "learned feature space"
        # We move dots to new positions where some previously far dots might become close
        move_animations = []
        for i, dot in enumerate(dots):
            # Generate a new target position
            if i == center_idx:
                new_pos = ORIGIN # Keep center at origin for clarity
            else:
                # Some dots move closer to center, some move further away
                # to show dynamic connectivity
                angle = np.random.uniform(0, 2 * PI)
                # Mixture of close and far to demonstrate weights
                dist = np.random.choice([0.8, 1.2, 4.0, 5.0], p=[0.2, 0.3, 0.3, 0.2])
                new_pos = np.array([dist * np.cos(angle), dist * np.sin(angle), 0])
            
            move_animations.append(dot.animate.move_to(new_pos))

        # Perform the transition
        # The updaters on 'connections' will automatically adjust stroke and opacity
        self.play(
            *move_animations,
            run_time=4,
            rate_func=bezier([0, 0, 1, 1])
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

        # Final jitter to show dynamic nature
        jitter_anims = []
        for dot in dots:
            jitter_pos = dot.get_center() + np.random.uniform(-0.4, 0.4, 3)
            jitter_anims.append(dot.animate.move_to(jitter_pos))
            
        self.play(*jitter_anims, run_time=2, rate_func=there_and_back)
        self.wait(1)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ObjectCondensation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # Configuration
        q_min = 0.1
        
        # Helper for interpolation since 'interpolate' was reported undefined
        def lerp(a, b, t):
            return (1 - t) * a + t * b

        # 1. Title and Formula
        formula = MathTex(r"q_i = \text{arctanh}(\beta_i) + q_{min}")
        formula.to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(formula))

        # 2. Create Grid of Hits (Dots)
        # Generate a grid and assign beta values
        dots = VGroup()
        condensation_points = []
        
        # Define a discrete color gradient for beta values
        # This avoids using interpolate_color directly if it's missing from namespace
        beta_colors = color_gradient([PURPLE, YELLOW], 100)
        
        rows, cols = 7, 12
        for i in range(rows):
            for j in range(cols):
                # Random-ish beta values
                beta = np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.7)
                
                # Designate two specific dots as high-beta condensation points
                if (i == 2 and j == 3) or (i == 5 and j == 8):
                    beta = 0.98
                
                x_pos = lerp(-5.5, 5.5, j / (cols - 1))
                y_pos = lerp(-3.0, 2.0, i / (rows - 1))
                
                # Pick color from our gradient
                color_idx = int(beta * 99)
                
                dot = Dot(
                    point=[x_pos, y_pos, 0],
                    radius=0.06,
                    color=beta_colors[color_idx]
                )
                dot.beta = beta
                dots.add(dot)
                
                if beta > 0.9:
                    condensation_points.append(dot)

        self.play(FadeIn(dots, lag_ratio=0.05), run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Animate Beta growth
        # Dots with higher beta grow larger
        growth_anims = []
        for dot in dots:
            target_scale = lerp(0.8, 3.5, dot.beta)
            growth_anims.append(dot.animate.scale(target_scale))
            
        self.play(*growth_anims, run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 4. Gravity Wells (Potential Field)
        wells = VGroup()
        for cp in condensation_points:
            # Concentric rings to simulate a gravity well / potential field
            well = VGroup(*[
                Circle(radius=r, stroke_width=1.5, stroke_opacity=1 - (r/2))
                .set_color(YELLOW)
                .move_to(cp.get_center())
                for r in np.arange(0.2, 2.2, 0.3)
            ])
            wells.add(well)
            
        self.play(Create(wells, lag_ratio=0.1), run_time=1.5)

        # 5. Pulling Mechanism
        # Hits move toward their closest high-beta condensation point
        pull_anims = []
        for dot in dots:
            if dot in condensation_points:
                continue
            
            # Find distances to condensation points
            dists = [np.linalg.norm(dot.get_center() - cp.get_center()) for cp in condensation_points]
            min_dist = min(dists)
            
            # If close enough, pull them in
            if min_dist < 3.5:
                nearest_cp = condensation_points[np.argmin(dists)]
                # Calculate target position (move 70% toward the center)
                start_pos = dot.get_center()
                end_pos = nearest_cp.get_center()
                target_pos = lerp(start_pos, end_pos, 0.7)
                pull_anims.append(dot.animate.move_to(target_pos))

        # Using 'smooth' rate function which is standard in Manim
        self.play(*pull_anims, run_time=2.5, rate_func=smooth)
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Showcase specific Charge calculation
        target_cp = condensation_points[1]
        # Calculate q_i = arctanh(beta) + q_min
        charge_val = np.arctanh(target_cp.beta) + q_min
        
        charge_tag = MathTex(f"q \approx {charge_val:.2f}")
        charge_tag.scale(0.8).next_to(target_cp, UR, buff=0.2)
        charge_tag.set_background_stroke(color=BLACK, width=4)
        
        self.play(
            Indicate(target_cp, scale_factor=1.5, color=WHITE),
            Write(charge_tag)
        )
        
        self.wait(3)

generated_code:
from manim import *
import numpy as np
import math

class ObjectCondensation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Formula Display
        formula = MathTex(r"q_i = \text{arctanh}(\beta_i) + q_{min}")
        formula.to_edge(UP)
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.wait(1)

        # 2. Setup Dots
        num_dots = 40
        dots = VGroup()
        beta_values = []
        
        # Determine some specific 'condensation points' with high beta
        condensation_indices = [5, 15, 25]
        
        for i in range(num_dots):
            # Random position in a 2D plane
            pos = [np.random.uniform(-5, 5), np.random.uniform(-3, 1), 0]
            dot = Dot(point=pos, radius=0.08, color=PURPLE)
            
            if i in condensation_indices:
                beta = np.random.uniform(0.8, 0.95)
            else:
                beta = np.random.uniform(0.1, 0.4)
                
            beta_values.append(beta)
            dots.add(dot)

        self.play(Create(dots))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Animate Beta growth: Size and Color (Purple to Yellow)
        animations = []
        for i, dot in enumerate(dots):
            beta = beta_values[i]
            # Manual color interpolation if interpolate_color is risky, 
            # but it is standard in Manim Community.
            target_color = interpolate_color(PURPLE, YELLOW, beta)
            target_scale = 1 + beta * 2.5
            animations.append(dot.animate.set_color(target_color).scale(target_scale))
        
        self.play(*animations, run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 4. Create Gravity Wells for high-beta dots
        wells = VGroup()
        for idx in condensation_indices:
            center_dot = dots[idx]
            # Use concentric circles for the gradient effect
            well = VGroup(*[
                Circle(radius=r, stroke_width=0, fill_opacity=0.15 - (r/3.0)*0.15)
                .set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=0.2 - (r/3.0)*0.2)
                .move_to(center_dot.get_center())
                for r in np.linspace(0.1, 3.0, 10)
            ])
            wells.add(well)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(wells))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Animate neighboring hits being pulled towards condensation points
        pull_animations = []
        for i, dot in enumerate(dots):
            if i in condensation_indices:
                continue
            
            # Find distances to all condensation points and find the closest one
            dists = [np.linalg.norm(dot.get_center() - dots[idx].get_center()) for idx in condensation_indices]
            min_dist_idx = np.argmin(dists)
            closest_cond_idx = condensation_indices[min_dist_idx]
            
            # Pull if within a reasonable range
            if dists[min_dist_idx] < 4.0:
                target_pos = dots[closest_cond_idx].get_center()
                # Move 70% of the way to the center
                pull_vector = (target_pos - dot.get_center()) * 0.7
                pull_animations.append(dot.animate.shift(pull_vector))

        if pull_animations:
            self.play(*pull_animations, run_time=3, rate_func=smooth)
        
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Final annotation for q calculation
        # Choose one condensation point to label
        sample_idx = condensation_indices[0]
        sample_beta = beta_values[sample_idx]
        q_val = math.atanh(sample_beta) + 0.1 # assuming q_min = 0.1
        
        charge_label = MathTex(
            f"q_{{{sample_idx}}} \\approx {q_val:.2f}", 
            color=YELLOW
        ).scale(0.8).next_to(dots[sample_idx], UP)
        
        self.play(Write(charge_label))
        self.play(Indicate(dots[sample_idx]))
        self.wait(3)

{
  "id": "concept-02-object-condensation",
  "voice": "To actually group these hits into particles, we use a technique called object condensation. Each hit predicts a confidence value, beta, which we treat like a physical charge. A high beta means the hit is likely the center, or 'condensation point,' of a particle. We then use this charge to create an attractive potential. Imagine these condensation points acting like gravity, pulling in all the hits that belong to that specific particle while pushing away the noise.",
  "goal": "Visualize the mechanism of beta-charge and the potential functions used for clustering.",
  "remarks": "Show a 2D plane of dots. Each dot grows in size and changes color from purple to bright yellow based on a simulated beta value. For dots with high beta, draw a concentric 'gravity well' (a gradient circle) using a Surface or a mesh. Show neighboring low-beta dots being physically pulled toward the center of these wells using an animation where they slide along the gradient lines. Use a MathTex label for the charge calculation q = arctanh(beta).",
  "formula": "q_i = \\text{arctanh}(\\beta_i) + q_{min}",
  "image_ref": null
}
```

generated_code:
from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ObjectCondensation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup the 2D plane of dots
        num_dots = 30
        # Initialize dots with a single purple color to avoid undefined color constants
        dots = VGroup(*[
            Dot(
                point=[np.random.uniform(-5, 5), np.random.uniform(-3, 3), 0],
                radius=0.08,
                color=PURPLE
            ) for _ in range(num_dots)
        ])
        
        # Select indices for condensation points (high beta) and noise
        indices = list(range(num_dots))
        np.random.shuffle(indices)
        cp_indices = indices[:3]
        noise_indices = indices[3:]

        self.add(dots)
        self.wait(1)

        # 2. Animate beta growth (color and size change)
        growth_animations = []
        condensation_points = []
        
        for i in range(num_dots):
            if i in cp_indices:
                # High beta dots turn yellow and grow
                growth_animations.append(
                    dots[i].animate.set_color(YELLOW).scale(2.5)
                )
                condensation_points.append(dots[i])
            else:
                # Low beta dots shrink slightly
                growth_animations.append(
                    dots[i].animate.scale(0.8)
                )
        
        self.play(*growth_animations, run_time=2)
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 3. Draw "Gravity Wells" (gradient circles) around high-beta dots
        wells = VGroup()
        for cp in condensation_points:
            # Concentric circles to simulate a gradient/gravity well
            well = VGroup(*[
                Circle(radius=r, stroke_width=0, fill_opacity=0.1)
                .set_fill(YELLOW)
                .move_to(cp.get_center())
                for r in [0.4, 0.8, 1.2]
            ])
            wells.add(well)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(wells))
        self.wait(0.5)

        # 4. Pull neighboring low-beta dots toward condensation points
        pull_animations = []
        for i in noise_indices:
            dot = dots[i]
            # Find closest condensation point center
            closest_cp = min(condensation_points, key=lambda cp: np.linalg.norm(cp.get_center() - dot.get_center()))
            dist = np.linalg.norm(closest_cp.get_center() - dot.get_center())
            
            # Pull dots that are within a certain range
            if dist < 4:
                # Move 70% of the way to the center
                target_pos = dot.get_center() + (closest_cp.get_center() - dot.get_center()) * 0.7
                pull_animations.append(dot.animate.move_to(target_pos))

        # Using 'smooth' instead of 'bezier' to ensure compatibility
        self.play(*pull_animations, run_time=2, rate_func=smooth)
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Display the charge calculation formula
        formula = MathTex(
            r"q_i = \text{arctanh}(\beta_i) + q_{min}",
            color=WHITE
        ).to_edge(UP)
        
        # Add a background rectangle for better legibility
        bg_rect = SurroundingRectangle(formula, color=BLACK, fill_opacity=0.8, buff=0.2)
        
        self.play(FadeIn(bg_rect), Write(formula))
        self.wait(3)

Use a ValueTracker to move a vertical line across the x-axis. from manim import *
import numpy as np

class EfficiencyVsOverlap(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Setup Axes (Pattern from Example 3)
        axes = Axes(
            x_range=[0, 5, 1],
            y_range=[0, 1.1, 0.2],
            x_length=9,
            y_length=5,
            axis_config={"include_tip": True}
        ).shift(DOWN * 0.5)
        
        # Labels
        x_label = axes.get_x_axis_label("Distance")
        y_label = axes.get_y_axis_label("Efficiency")

        # 2. Curves (Pattern from Example 3)
        # Using named functions to ensure stability in the parser
        def ml_func(val):
            return 0.9

        def std_func(val):
            # Starts high, drops sharply toward 0 at small values
            if val < 0.1:
                return 0
            return 0.9 * (1.0 - np.exp(-1.5 * (val - 0.1)))

        ml_curve = axes.plot(ml_func, color=BLUE, x_range=[0, 5])
        std_curve = axes.plot(std_func, color=RED, x_range=[0, 5]).set_stroke(dash_length=0.1)

        ml_tex = MathTex("ML Model", color=BLUE).scale(0.8).move_to(axes.c2p(4, 1.0))
        std_tex = MathTex("Standard Algorithm", color=RED).scale(0.8).move_to(axes.c2p(4, 0.6))

        # 3. Dynamic Elements (Pattern from Example 2)
        dist_tracker = ValueTracker(4.0)

        # Vertical indicator line
        v_line = Line(axes.c2p(4, 0), axes.c2p(4, 0.9), color=YELLOW)
        def v_line_updater(mob):
            curr_x = dist_tracker.get_value()
            new_line = Line(
                axes.c2p(curr_x, 0),
                axes.c2p(curr_x, 0.9),
                color=YELLOW,
                stroke_width=2
            )
            mob.become(new_line)
        v_line.add_updater(v_line_updater)

        # Particle shower icons (Simplified as circles for stability)
        shower_l = Circle(radius=0.2, color=WHITE, fill_opacity=0.5)
        shower_r = Circle(radius=0.2, color=WHITE, fill_opacity=0.5)

        def shower_l_updater(mob):
            curr_dist = dist_tracker.get_value()
            # Move showers closer as tracker value decreases
            mob.move_to(axes.c2p(2.5 - curr_dist * 0.4, 0.4))
            
        def shower_r_updater(mob):
            curr_dist = dist_tracker.get_value()
            mob.move_to(axes.c2p(2.5 + curr_dist * 0.4, 0.4))

        shower_l.add_updater(shower_l_updater)
        shower_r.add_updater(shower_r_updater)

        # 4. Animation Sequence
        self.add(axes, x_label, y_label)
        self.play(Create(ml_curve), Write(ml_tex))
        self.play(Create(std_curve), Write(std_tex))
        self.wait(0.5)

        self.add(v_line, shower_l, shower_r)
        
        # Animate the distance closing
        # As the tracker moves to the left, the vertical line moves 
        # and the shower icons merge.
        self.play(
            dist_tracker.animate.set_value(0.1),
            run_time=6,
            rate_func=linear
        )
        
        self.play(Indicate(ml_curve))
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *

class ConceptEmbedding(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Coordinate Indicators (Mimicking a coordinate plane since NumberPlane is disallowed)
        x_axis = Line(LEFT * 5, RIGHT * 5, color=GREY_B)
        y_axis = Line(DOWN * 3, UP * 3, color=GREY_B)
        self.add(x_axis, y_axis)

        # 2. Text Labels for Concepts
        # Positioning them to illustrate proximity in semantic space
        dl_label = Text("Deep Learning").scale(0.5).move_to([2, 2, 0])
        nn_label = Text("Neural Networks").scale(0.5).move_to([2.5, 1.3, 0])
        ro_label = Text("Robotics").scale(0.5).move_to([-3, -1.5, 0])

        self.play(Write(dl_label), Write(nn_label), Write(ro_label))
        self.wait(1)

        # 3. Transform Text Labels into Dot Objects
        dl_dot = Dot([2, 2, 0], color=BLUE)
        nn_dot = Dot([2.5, 1.3, 0], color=BLUE)
        ro_dot = Dot([-3, -1.5, 0], color=RED)

        self.play(
            Transform(dl_label, dl_dot),
            Transform(nn_label, nn_dot),
            Transform(ro_label, ro_dot)
        )
        self.wait(1)

        # 4. Show distance (Using Line as DashedLine is disallowed)
        # Deep Learning and Neural Networks are close
        dist_near = Line(dl_dot.get_center(), nn_dot.get_center(), color=YELLOW)
        # Robotics is far from Deep Learning
        dist_far = Line(dl_dot.get_center(), ro_dot.get_center(), color=WHITE)

        self.play(Create(dist_near))
        self.play(Create(dist_far))
        self.wait(1)

        # 5. Display Formula and Clear Scene
        self.play(
            FadeOut(dl_label), FadeOut(nn_label), FadeOut(ro_label),
            FadeOut(dist_near), FadeOut(dist_far),
            FadeOut(x_axis), FadeOut(y_axis)
        )

        formula = MathTex(r"v_{phrase} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} v_{i}")
        self.play(Write(formula))
        self.play(formula.animate.to_edge(UP))
        self.wait(1)

        # 6. Averaging logic: Machine + Learning = Machine Learning
        # Define vector positions
        v_machine_pos = [-2.5, -0.5, 0]
        v_learning_pos = [1.5, 1.5, 0]
        # Calculate average position: ([-2.5+1.5]/2, [-0.5+1.5]/2) = (-0.5, 0.5)
        v_avg_pos = [-0.5, 0.5, 0]

        # Use Arrow as Vector is disallowed
        arrow_m = Arrow(ORIGIN, v_machine_pos, buff=0, color=GREEN)
        lab_m = Text("Machine").scale(0.5).next_to(v_machine_pos, DOWN)

        arrow_l = Arrow(ORIGIN, v_learning_pos, buff=0, color=ORANGE)
        lab_l = Text("Learning").scale(0.5).next_to(v_learning_pos, UP)

        self.play(Create(arrow_m), Write(lab_m))
        self.play(Create(arrow_l), Write(lab_l))
        self.wait(1)

        # Merge vectors and words into the average central vector
        arrow_avg = Arrow(ORIGIN, v_avg_pos, buff=0, color=YELLOW)
        lab_avg = Text("Machine Learning").scale(0.6).next_to(v_avg_pos, RIGHT)

        self.play(
            Transform(arrow_m, arrow_avg),
            Transform(arrow_l, arrow_avg),
            Transform(lab_m, lab_avg),
            Transform(lab_l, lab_avg)
        )
        self.wait(2)

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class ConceptAnimation(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Manual Axes (Using Line since Axes was flagged)
        x_axis = Line(4 * LEFT, 1 * RIGHT, color=WHITE).shift(2 * LEFT)
        y_axis = Line(2.5 * DOWN, 2.5 * UP, color=WHITE).shift(2 * LEFT)
        origin_point = x_axis.get_center()
        self.add(x_axis, y_axis)

        # 2. Vectors A and B
        # Vector A is static
        vec_a_end = origin_point + 2.0 * RIGHT
        vec_a = Line(origin_point, vec_a_end, color=BLUE).add_tip()
        label_a = MathTex(r"\mathbf{A}", color=BLUE).next_to(vec_a, UP, buff=0.1)
        self.add(vec_a, label_a)

        # Vector B is dynamic - using a Dot's x-coordinate to track angle
        angle_tracker = Dot(point=[PI / 3, 0, 0]).set_opacity(0)
        self.add(angle_tracker)

        vec_b = Line(origin_point, origin_point + 2.0 * RIGHT, color=GREEN).add_tip()
        
        def update_vec_b(m):
            angle = angle_tracker.get_x()
            new_end = origin_point + np.array([2.0 * np.cos(angle), 2.0 * np.sin(angle), 0])
            m.become(Line(origin_point, new_end, color=GREEN).add_tip())
        
        vec_b.add_updater(update_vec_b)
        
        label_b = MathTex(r"\mathbf{B}", color=GREEN)
        def update_label_b(m):
            m.next_to(vec_b.get_end(), UR, buff=0.1)
        label_b.add_updater(update_label_b)
        
        self.add(vec_b, label_b)

        # 3. Angle Arc and Similarity text
        # Using Arc instead of Angle as it was flagged
        arc = Arc(radius=0.6, start_angle=0, angle=PI / 3, arc_center=origin_point, color=YELLOW)
        def update_arc(m):
            angle = angle_tracker.get_x()
            m.become(Arc(radius=0.6, start_angle=0, angle=angle, arc_center=origin_point, color=YELLOW))
        arc.add_updater(update_arc)

        theta_label = MathTex(r"\theta", color=YELLOW)
        def update_theta_label(m):
            angle = angle_tracker.get_x()
            m.move_to(origin_point + np.array([0.9 * np.cos(angle/2), 0.9 * np.sin(angle/2), 0]))
        theta_label.add_updater(update_theta_label)

        sim_formula = MathTex(r"\text{similarity} = \cos(\theta)", font_size=36).to_edge(UP)
        self.add(arc, theta_label, sim_formula)

        # 4. Matrix Grid
        grid = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.6).set_stroke(GRAY, 1).set_fill(BLUE, opacity=0.1)
            for _ in range(25)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(5, 5, buff=0.05).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1)
        grid_label = Text("Similarity Matrix", font_size=24).next_to(grid, UP)
        self.add(grid, grid_label)

        # Middle cell responds to alignment
        active_cell = grid[12]
        def update_cell(m):
            val = np.cos(angle_tracker.get_x())
            if val > 0:
                m.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=val)
            else:
                m.set_fill(BLACK, opacity=abs(val))
        active_cell.add_updater(update_cell)

        # 5. Animations
        # Rotate B to align with A
        self.play(angle_tracker.animate.set_x(5 * DEGREES), run_time=3)
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Rotate B away from A
        self.play(angle_tracker.animate.set_x(120 * DEGREES), run_time=3)
        self.wait(1)

        # Randomize other cells for visual effect
        self.play(*[
            c.animate.set_fill(WHITE if np.random.rand() > 0.7 else BLUE, opacity=np.random.rand())
            for i, c in enumerate(grid) if i != 12
        ])
        
        self.play(angle_tracker.animate.set_x(0), run_time=2)
        self.wait(2)

        # Cleanup updaters
        vec_b.clear_updaters()
        label_b.clear_updaters()
        arc.clear_updaters()
        theta_label.clear_updaters()
        active_cell.clear_updaters()

As the vectors on the left align, the corresponding cell in the matrix should glow brighter (white/yellow) and as they diverge, it should turn dark (blue/black).",

from manim import *
import numpy as np

class CosineSimilarityMatrix(Scene):
    def construct(self):
        # 1. Manual Setup for Axes (Avoiding 'Axes' as it was flagged)
        origin = LEFT * 3.5 + DOWN * 1
        x_axis = Line(origin, origin + RIGHT * 3, color=WHITE).add_tip()
        y_axis = Line(origin, origin + UP * 3, color=WHITE).add_tip()
        
        # 2. Tracking state (Avoiding 'ValueTracker' as it was flagged)
        # Using a hidden Mobject's coordinate to store the angle value
        angle_tracker = Dot(point=[2.0, 0, 0]).hide()
        self.add(angle_tracker)
        
        # 3. Vectors (Using Line + tip instead of 'Vector')
        # Vector A: Fixed position
        angle_a = 0.5  # Fixed angle for vector A
        vec_a_end = origin + np.array([2.5 * np.cos(angle_a), 2.5 * np.sin(angle_a), 0])
        vector_A = Line(origin, vec_a_end, color=BLUE).add_tip()
        label_A = MathTex(r"\mathbf{A}", color=BLUE).next_to(vec_a_end, UR, buff=0.1)
        
        # Vector B: Dynamic, controlled by the angle_tracker
        vector_B = Line(origin, origin + RIGHT, color=GREEN).add_tip()
        def update_vector_b(mob):
            theta = angle_tracker.get_x()
            new_end = origin + np.array([2.5 * np.cos(theta), 2.5 * np.sin(theta), 0])
            mob.become(Line(origin, new_end, color=GREEN).add_tip())
        vector_B.add_updater(update_vector_b)
        
        label_B = MathTex(r"\mathbf{B}", color=GREEN)
        def update_label_b(mob):
            end = vector_B.get_end()
            diff = end - origin
            norm = np.sqrt(np.sum(diff**2))
            if norm > 0:
                direction = diff / norm
            else:
                direction = RIGHT
            mob.move_to(end + direction * 0.3)
        label_B.add_updater(update_label_b)
        
        # 4. Angle Arc (Avoiding 'Angle' as it was flagged)
        angle_arc = Arc(radius=0.7, arc_center=origin, color=YELLOW)
        def update_arc(mob):
            theta_b = angle_tracker.get_x()
            start = min(angle_a, theta_b)
            diff = abs(angle_a - theta_b)
            mob.become(Arc(radius=0.7, start_angle=start, angle=diff, arc_center=origin, color=YELLOW))
        angle_arc.add_updater(update_arc)
        
        # 5. Formula and Similarity Matrix
        # Displaying the mathematical context
        formula = MathTex(
            r"\text{similarity} = \cos(\theta) = \frac{\mathbf{A} \cdot \mathbf{B}}{\|\mathbf{A}\| \|\mathbf{B}\|}",
            font_size=32
        ).to_edge(UP, buff=0.5)
        
        # Matrix grid (5x5)
        matrix = VGroup(*[
            Square(side_length=0.4, fill_opacity=0.1, stroke_width=1).set_stroke(GRAY, 1)
            for _ in range(25)
        ]).arrange_in_grid(rows=5, cols=5, buff=0.1).to_edge(RIGHT, buff=1)
        
        matrix_title = Text("Similarity Matrix", font_size=20).next_to(matrix, UP)
        
        # Similarity Value Text
        sim_val_text = MathTex("1.00", color=YELLOW).next_to(matrix, DOWN, buff=0.5)
        def update_sim_val(mob):
            theta_b = angle_tracker.get_x()
            val = np.cos(theta_b - angle_a)
            mob.become(MathTex(f"{val:.2f}", color=YELLOW).next_to(matrix, DOWN, buff=0.5))
        sim_val_text.add_updater(update_sim_val)

        # 6. Linking alignment to matrix brightness
        # We'll use the center cell (index 12) to represent the current A/B pair
        matrix[12].set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=0)
        def update_cell_glow(mob):
            theta_b = angle_tracker.get_x()
            val = np.cos(theta_b - angle_a)
            # Map similarity (-1 to 1) to opacity (0 to 1)
            alpha = max(0, min(1, val))
            mob.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=alpha)
        matrix[12].add_updater(update_cell_glow)
        
        # Adding components to scene
        self.add(x_axis, y_axis, vector_A, label_A, vector_B, label_B, angle_arc, formula, matrix, matrix_title, sim_val_text)
        
        # 7. Animation Sequences
        # Start with vectors divergent
        self.play(angle_tracker.animate.set_x(2.5), run_time=0.5)
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Rotate Vector B to align with A
        # Voice: "If two vectors point in exactly the same direction, the cosine is one..."
        self.play(
            angle_tracker.animate.set_x(angle_a),
            run_time=5,
            rate_func=lambda t: t # Manual linear rate function
        )
        self.wait(1)
        
        # Diversify other cells to show a completed heat map
        self.play(
            *[m.animate.set_fill(YELLOW, opacity=np.random.rand()*0.7) 
              for i, m in enumerate(matrix) if i != 12],
            run_time=2
        )
        
        self.wait(2)

  "id": "concept-02-regularization-tradeoff",
  "voice": "As we add more players and more stats, the model gets more information, but it also risks getting lost in the noise. This is the curse of dimensionality. While simple models like K-Nearest Neighbors struggle as the feature space grows, Lasso and Ridge regression excel. They apply a penalty to the size of the coefficients, effectively telling the model: 'only pay attention to the stats that actually drive wins.'",
  "goal": "Explain why Lasso and Ridge regression outperform other models as dimensionality increases.",
  "remarks": "Create an Axes plot where the x-axis is 'Number of Players' (1 to 10) and the y-axis is 'Mean Squared Error'. Recreate the curves from img-0. Plot the KNN line in red, showing it dipping then rising sharply as it overfits. Plot the Lasso/Ridge lines in green, showing a steady decline. To visualize the penalty, show the Lasso cost function equation. As the lambda term increases, show a bar chart of 150 coefficients shrinking, with many of them hitting zero (vanishing) in the Lasso case to highlight feature selection.",
  "formula": "J(\\theta) = \\|y - X\\theta\\|^2 + \\lambda \\|\\theta\\|_1",
  "image_ref": "img-0.jpeg"
}
